summary: FQ-09M lightweight action animation (commit 039308d): one reusable self-freeing effect node (scripts/fx/action_fx.gd - five deterministic stepped-10Hz kinds under 0.4s in the action_fx group, null-safe static spawn) plus a stepped tool-swing arc in player._draw (pick/axe glyph cycling raise/mid/strike at six pose-steps/s toward the mining target; swing_phase() hook). Wired: placement pulse, attunement cast ring, player/enemy hit sparks, collapse/respawn/death dust, and one _craft_confirm_fx choke point covering all four hall forges and hand crafting. Presentation only - mining frames, drops, damage math, and saves stay pinned by pre-existing checks; 7 fq09m_* smoke checks bring the suite to 217/217. Same run, separate commit (9b85b3f): intake of the Codex lane's adaptive-score assets per the FQ-09U0 contract - 4 context loops, 6 phase-locked stems, 5 stingers, the m8patch source, and repeatable render/verify tooling; mechanically verified (loops exactly 2,560,000 samples @ 48kHz, stingers <8s, 63 stem combos below full scale); runtime playback NOT wired and operator listening approval PENDING (the two remaining FQ-09U1 gates are the Godot audio spike and that approval). Operator-side .rar packaging archives ignore-listed. Haiku verifier NO FINDINGS across both change sets; one concurrency note recorded (Codex hardened two in-flight smoke checks - kept after review; lanes to re-separate).
scope: FQ-09M per docs/FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.md (no timing/drops/damage/save/input changes, no animation framework) plus evidence-preserving intake of Codex-lane FQ-09U assets. No FQ-09U1 runtime work.
next_action: FQ-09U1 once its gates are met: operator listens to the suite; Codex delivers the Godot 4.6 audio spike evidence.
